title: Thelypteris magna
taxonomy: Thelypteris magna is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Thelypteris, a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Thelypteris magna is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the division Pteridophyta. The plant's classification also includes the class Polypodiopsida and the order Blechnales. This comprehensive classification highlights the unique characteristics and evolutionary history of the Thelypteris magna fern.

intro: Thelypteris magna, also known as the large wood fern, is a species of fern native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris magna include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris magna is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
